html:
SSB8 / Southern Score Builders

Southern Score is not the 5x forward P/E data-center moonshot described in the old draft. It is better than that in business quality, but less absurdly cheap. At RM0.540 on 18 May 2026, the stock is about RM1.23 billion of equity value on 2.27 billion shares, with the next quarterly result expected on 28 May. The market is valuing it at roughly 16 to 18x FY2026 earnings, 12 to 13x FY2027 earnings, and below 11x FY2028 if the current order-book conversion happens.

That is still attractive because the market label is wrong. SSB8 is treated as a Malaysian construction counter with related-party project flow and high receivables. The better label is a small, asset-light construction manager becoming a higher-spec electrical, data-center and regulated-facility contractor. The 51%-owned SJEE acquisition gave it specialized power-distribution exposure for data centers. The Nova Pharma takeover adds life-sciences engineering capability, which matters because hospital, pharma and biotech projects have more compliance complexity and better margins than vanilla residential construction.

The correction to the old thesis is important: this is not a RM10 billion backlog story. The latest credible order-book number is about RM1.6 billion, with data-center projects around 27% of the book and residential still around 70%. The bullish point is not the absolute backlog. It is the direction of mix. On 31 March, SJEE secured RM189 million of data-center electrical works, including a RM150 million project and a RM39 million expansion to an existing project, bringing that project to RM115 million. Research estimates put FY2026 year-to-date wins around RM408 million to RM424 million after those awards, with tender pipelines still visible in data centers, Radium Hospital, PV and Radium-linked property work.

The near-term stumble is real but probably not thesis-breaking. 2Q FY2026 revenue was RM96.3 million and profit attributable to shareholders was RM10.4 million, down sequentially from RM15.0 million in 1Q, with 1H FY2026 PATAMI at RM25.4 million. The issue was cement and aggregate supply disruption plus diesel and haulage cost pressure, not demand disappearance. Phillip cut FY2026 earnings after this disruption, but still forecasts revenue rising from RM221 million in FY2025 to RM542 million in FY2026, RM742 million in FY2027 and RM879 million in FY2028, with net profit rising from RM40 million to RM68 million, RM93 million and RM113 million. Apex is a little higher at RM75.5 million FY2026 PATAMI and RM104 million FY2027.

At today’s price, that means the stock is not cheap on stale trailing numbers but is cheap enough on forward economics if SSB8 really becomes a higher-margin technical contractor. Net cash of roughly RM60 million helps, but it is not the reason to own the stock. The reason is earnings compounding: FY2025 to FY2028 net profit can plausibly triple while the stock is still priced as a small construction name.

The most interesting variant perception is that related-party dependence is both the risk and the engine. The Gan family-linked Platinum Victory and Radium relationships make investors uncomfortable, correctly. They also provide recurring work that a normal small contractor would not have. If the company can use that base load to prove M&E, data-center and Nova Pharma execution, the market can rerate the earnings from “lumpy related-party contractor” to “specialized infrastructure contractor with repeat customers.”

Retail sentiment is not undiscovered. Local forums and technical blogs are already talking about data-center upside, breakouts and the RM0.56 to RM0.60 range. That means the easy surprise is gone. But the retail conversation still looks mostly narrative and chart-driven, not model-driven. The market is not fully paying for FY2027 to FY2028 earnings if new awards convert and margins hold.

The next signposts are compressed. Q3 FY2026 on or around 28 May must show that 2Q was a timing disruption, not margin decay. A clean print with revenue catch-up, stable double-digit net margin, and no receivables blowout would matter more than another promotional data-center headline. Then come the real rerating catalysts: Radium Hospital main works around mid-2026, existing data-center customer Phase 2, potential Phase 3, and the Port Dickson United States-based hyperscaler tender expected in 3Q 2026. Any partial conversion of that pipeline would make the current RM1.6 billion order book look like a staging point rather than a peak.

The bear case is also clear. Receivables are large, debtor days have been high, and the model assumes working-capital normalization. SJEE is only 51% owned, so investors should not capitalize 100% of its revenue or profit. Construction cost inflation can still hurt because some operating costs are not fully pass-through. Malaysia is also becoming more selective on data-center approvals because of power and water usage, and that can delay projects even if long-term demand is real. Finally, insider-linked disposals in early 2026 and the concentration of control deserve a discount.

The stock is a buy only if one is willing to underwrite execution through FY2027, not if one needs immediate proof from the last quarter. The best version of the thesis is that SSB8 is early in a three-part transition: residential construction manager, then data-center electrical subcontractor, then higher-spec technical contractor for data centers, hospitals, pharma and advanced facilities. The market has started to see part two but is not yet paying for part three.

Bear target: RM0.38, about 30% downside from RM0.540. This assumes Q3 does not recover, FY2027 PATAMI stalls near RM70 million, receivables remain heavy, and the market assigns only 12x earnings to a contractor with governance and working-capital concerns.

Base target: RM0.80, about 48% upside. This assumes FY2027 PATAMI around RM100 million, RM1.0 billion-plus annual replenishment resumes, data-center and Nova Pharma margins hold, and the stock trades at 17 to 18x FY2027 earnings, in line with current broker targets.

Bull target: RM1.20, about 122% upside. This requires a major Port Dickson or equivalent hyperscaler win, Radium Hospital conversion, order book above RM2.5 billion, FY2028 PATAMI around RM150 million, and a rerating to 18x forward earnings as the market stops valuing SSB8 as a plain construction stock.
